revision.text:
{{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=CodeEditorAssist |desc=<onlyinclude>Allows customizing the [[mw:CodeEditor|code editor]], e.g. changing the color theme and enabling live autocompletion</onlyinclude> |screenshot=CodeEditorAssist screenshot.png |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. |source=[[User:Nardog/CodeEditorAssist.js]] }}
Did you know that pressing {{key press|Ctrl|,}} in the [[mw:CodeEditor|code editor]] for CSS, JavaScript, JSON, Lua, and edit filters opens a configuration panel where you can customize the highlighting theme, keybindings, font size, wrapping style, cursor style, indentation method, undo buffers, and even live autocompletion? I know, right? Or you did but you wished you could have kept your preferences across pages? Right, right. Well, you've come to the right place.
CodeEditorAssist will allow you to save your code editor preferences and automatically apply them every time you open the editor. It also adds a handy settings button to the code editor toolbar.
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.js|global.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/CodeEditorAssist.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==Saving settings== Settings are saved to your global preferences. While saved settings are automatically loaded every time you open a page with the code editor, you have to hit Save in order to save them in the first place (so any changes made to the settings afterwards will not be saved, including through the default toggle buttons on the toolbar). The options "Mode" and "Read-only" are not saved.
==See also== *[https://ace.c9.io/#nav=howto Ace documentation] *[[phab:T261118]]
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was 1 revision imported from [[:wikipedia:User:Nardog/CodeEditorAssist]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=CodeEditorAssist |desc=<onlyinclude>Allows customizing the [[mw:CodeEditor|code editor]], e.g. changing the color theme and enabling live autocompletion</onlyinclude> |screenshot=CodeEditorAssist screenshot.png |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. |source=[[User:Nardog/CodeEditorAssist.js]] }}
Did you know that pressing {{key press|Ctrl|,}} in the [[mw:CodeEditor|code editor]] for CSS, JavaScript, JSON, Lua, and edit filters opens a configuration panel where you can customize the highlighting theme, keybindings, font size, wrapping style, cursor style, indentation method, undo buffers, and even live autocompletion? I know, right? Or you did but you wished you could have kept your preferences across pages? Right, right. Well, you've come to the right place.
CodeEditorAssist will allow you to save your code editor preferences and automatically apply them every time you open the editor. It also adds a handy settings button to the code editor toolbar.
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.js|global.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/CodeEditorAssist.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==Saving settings== Settings are saved to your global preferences. While saved settings are automatically loaded every time you open a page with the code editor, you have to hit Save in order to save them in the first place (so any changes made to the settings afterwards will not be saved, including through the default toggle buttons on the toolbar). The options "Mode" and "Read-only" are not saved.
==See also== *[https://ace.c9.io/#nav=howto Ace documentation] *[[phab:T261118]]
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}

{{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=CodeEditorAssist |desc=<onlyinclude>Allows customizing the [[mw:CodeEditor|code editor]], e.g. changing the color theme and enabling live autocompletion</onlyinclude> |screenshot=CodeEditorAssist screenshot.png |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. |source=[[User:Nardog/CodeEditorAssist.js]] }}
Did you know that pressing {{key press|Ctrl|,}} in the [[mw:CodeEditor|code editor]] for CSS, JavaScript, JSON, Lua, and edit filters opens a configuration panel where you can customize the highlighting theme, keybindings, font size, wrapping style, cursor style, indentation method, undo buffers, and even live autocompletion? I know, right? Or you did but you wished you could have kept your preferences across pages? Right, right. Well, you've come to the right place.
CodeEditorAssist will allow you to save your code editor preferences and automatically apply them every time you open the editor. It also adds a handy settings button to the code editor toolbar.
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.js|global.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/CodeEditorAssist.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==Saving settings== Settings are saved to your global preferences. While saved settings are automatically loaded every time you open a page with the code editor, you have to hit Save in order to save them in the first place (so any changes made to the settings afterwards will not be saved, including through the default toggle buttons on the toolbar). The options "Mode" and "Read-only" are not saved.
==See also== *[https://ace.c9.io/#nav=howto Ace documentation] *[[phab:T261118]]
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}[[Category:reimport]]

{{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=CodeEditorAssist |desc=<onlyinclude>Allows customizing the [[mw:CodeEditor|code editor]], e.g. changing the color theme and disabling live autocompletion</onlyinclude> |screenshot=CodeEditorAssist screenshot.png |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. |source=[[User:Nardog/CodeEditorAssist.js]] }}
Did you know that pressing {{key press|Ctrl|,}} in the [[mw:CodeEditor|code editor]] for CSS, JavaScript, JSON, Lua, and edit filters opens a configuration panel where you can customize the highlighting theme, keybindings, font size, wrapping style, cursor style, indentation method, undo buffers, and live autocompletion? I know, right? Or you did but you wished you could have kept your preferences across pages? Right, right. Well, you've come to the right place.
CodeEditorAssist will allow you to save your code editor preferences and automatically apply them every time you open the editor. It also adds a handy settings button to the code editor toolbar.
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.js|global.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/CodeEditorAssist.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==Saving settings== Settings are saved to your global preferences. While saved settings are automatically loaded every time you open a page with the code editor, you have to hit Save in order to save them in the first place (so any changes made to the settings afterwards will not be saved, including through the default toggle buttons on the toolbar). The options "Mode" and "Read-only" are not saved.
==See also== *[https://ace.c9.io/#nav=howto Ace documentation] *[[phab:T261118]]
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}

{{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=CodeEditorAssist |desc=<onlyinclude>Allows customizing the [[mw:CodeEditor|code editor]], e.g. changing the color theme and disabling live autocompletion</onlyinclude> |screenshot=CodeEditorAssist screenshot.png |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. |source=[[User:Nardog/CodeEditorAssist.js]] }}
Did you know that pressing {{key press|Ctrl|,}} in the [[mw:CodeEditor|code editor]] for CSS, JavaScript, JSON, Lua, and edit filters opens a configuration panel where you can customize the highlighting theme, keybindings, font size, wrapping style, cursor style, indentation method, undo buffers, and live autocompletion? I know, right? Or you did but you wished you could have kept your preferences across pages? Right, right. Well, you've come to the right place.
CodeEditorAssist will allow you to save your code editor preferences and automatically apply them every time you open the editor. It also adds a handy settings button to the code editor toolbar.
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.js|global.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/CodeEditorAssist.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==Saving settings== Settings are saved to your global preferences. While saved settings are automatically loaded every time you open a page with the code editor, you have to hit Save in order to save them in the first place (so any changes made to the settings afterwards will not be saved, including through the default toggle buttons on the toolbar). The options "Mode" and "Read-only" are not saved.
==See also== *[https://ace.c9.io/#nav=howto Ace documentation] *[[phab:T261118]]
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Nardog%2FCodeEditorAssist here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24796114356|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!-- History offloaded: due to miraheze stability concerns, the edit history of this page has been offloaded to the XML archive. See https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Nardog%2FCodeEditorAssist for full history. Last refreshed: [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24796114356|commit triggered pipeline]] --> {{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=CodeEditorAssist |desc=<onlyinclude>Allows customizing the [[mw:CodeEditor|code editor]], e.g. changing the color theme and disabling live autocompletion</onlyinclude> |screenshot=CodeEditorAssist screenshot.png |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. |source=[[User:Nardog/CodeEditorAssist.js]] }}
Did you know that pressing {{key press|Ctrl|,}} in the [[mw:CodeEditor|code editor]] for CSS, JavaScript, JSON, Lua, and edit filters opens a configuration panel where you can customize the highlighting theme, keybindings, font size, wrapping style, cursor style, indentation method, undo buffers, and live autocompletion? I know, right? Or you did but you wished you could have kept your preferences across pages? Right, right. Well, you've come to the right place.
CodeEditorAssist will allow you to save your code editor preferences and automatically apply them every time you open the editor. It also adds a handy settings button to the code editor toolbar.
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.js|global.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/CodeEditorAssist.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==Saving settings== Settings are saved to your global preferences. While saved settings are automatically loaded every time you open a page with the code editor, you have to hit Save in order to save them in the first place (so any changes made to the settings afterwards will not be saved, including through the default toggle buttons on the toolbar). The options "Mode" and "Read-only" are not saved.
==See also== *[https://ace.c9.io/#nav=howto Ace documentation] *[[phab:T261118]]
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}
